_comment: Staging file for Claude-in-session AI summaries. See pipeline/enrichers/prompts.py for the contract. Run `python -m pipeline.enrichers.summarize` to stamp these onto district profiles.

37-68338.overview: San Diego Unified is a large K-12 unified district serving 3,484 students with active IEPs, including 560 autistic students. About 85% of autistic students spend 80% or more of the school day in general-education settings, slightly below the 92% rate across all disability categories. Only 1.3% of autistic students are placed in fully separate special-education settings.
37-68338.what_this_means_for_parents: For an autistic child, the data shows inclusion-leaning placement is typical, with roughly 85 of every 100 autistic students learning alongside general-education peers for at least 80% of the school day. Academic outcomes for Students with Disabilities — a broader group that includes autism but is not autism-specific — sit 68 points below standard in English and 96 points below in math, while the all-students baseline is 12 points above standard in English and 19 points below in math. Chronic absenteeism and suspension rates for SWD (28% and 4%) run higher than the all-students baseline (19% and 2%). Because this is the largest district in the county, district-wide averages don't capture variation between individual schools.

37-68296.overview: Poway Unified is a K-12 unified district serving 5,511 students with active IEPs, including 1,314 autistic students. About 43% of autistic students spend 80% or more of the school day in general-education settings, lower than the 66% rate across all disability categories. Around 3.5% of autistic students are placed in fully separate special-education environments.
37-68296.what_this_means_for_parents: For an autistic child, the data shows full-day general-education placement is less common than partial-day or specialized placement, with under half of autistic students in general-education settings 80% or more of the school day. District-wide academic performance is strong — 56 points above standard in English and 40 points above in math for all students — though Students with Disabilities (a broader group that includes autism but is not autism-specific) sit 44 points below standard in English and 66 points below in math. Chronic absenteeism for SWD is 15%, roughly double the 9% rate for all students, and SWD suspensions are 3% compared to 1% district-wide. Inclusion rates are noticeably lower for autistic students (43%) than for the broader SWD group (66%), so placement patterns appear to differ by disability category.

37-67983.overview: Borrego Springs Unified is a small K-12 rural district serving 614 students with active IEPs, including 15 autistic students. All 15 autistic students and the entire SWD population are reported to spend 80% or more of the school day in general-education settings, with no students in fully separate special-education placements.
37-67983.what_this_means_for_parents: With only 15 autistic students reported district-wide, the 100% general-education inclusion rate reflects a very small enrollment — a single placement change can shift the percentage substantially, so this number should be interpreted with caution. Academic outcomes are below standard across all measured groups: all students are 47 points below in English and 85 points below in math, and Students with Disabilities (a broader group that includes autism but is not autism-specific) sit 132 points below standard in English and 182 points below in math. Chronic absenteeism is 25% for SWD versus 24% overall, and SWD suspensions (3%) are similar to the all-students rate (4%). These numbers reflect the 2024-25 school year for academic and attendance data and the prior school year for special-education placement.
